key_insight: WeldMamba predicts future molten-pool, wire, and keyhole segmentation from past images plus process signals to compensate for welding control delay.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable real-time perception for closed-loop laser wire-feed welding, where delayed sensing can otherwise degrade control and defect prevention.
method: Physics-guided spatiotemporal state space model for lookahead segmentation
Fuses coaxial grayscale history, welding parameters, and wire-state electrical signals
Adds horizon-conditioned prediction, motion-aware decoding, and geometry/consistency losses
evidence_signal: On a 43-sequence laser welding dataset, the method achieved 74.63% mIoU at 500 ms lookahead, with ablations showing gains from temporal history, patch-level state space modeling, and keyhole motion awareness.
watch_item: Performance depends on accurate synchronization of images, process parameters, and electrical signals; sensor drift or timing misalignment could reduce lookahead reliability.
paper_title: Physics-Guided Spatiotemporal State Space Modeling for Lookahead Molten Pool Segmentation in Laser Wire-Feed Welding
